event_derived.mechanism: Queries EventStore for coordination.path.claimed and coordination.path.released events, then computes active = claims ∖ releases via set subtraction
flaws: limit: 50 — misses old claims if more than 50 events exist
Event-based deduction is lossy; if the coordination.path.claimed event was pruned or never stored, the result is wrong
No status concept — just set math on event type names
assumes coordination writes to EventStore, but coordination actually writes to CoordinationReservationTable directly
Does not query CoordinationReservationTable at all

coordination_table_primary.mechanism: Primary path queries CoordinationReservationTable via Coordination.getSessionReservations(); fallback extracts file paths from recent events
strengths: Primary path reads from authoritative CoordinationReservationTable
Has graceful fallback to event-derived paths when no reservations exist

get_session_reservations.mechanism: Drizzle query against CoordinationReservationTable filtering by session_id and status='reserved'

smoking_guns.alignment_gap: GetClaimContextTool derives claims from EventStore events (coordination.path.claimed/released), but the coordination system stores claims in CoordinationReservationTable via Drizzle. The two sources may diverge if events are pruned, never written, or written under different eventType names.
smoking_guns.event_type_mismatch: packet.ts line 196 describes the authoritative source as 'prefer coordination table, fall back to events', while tools.ts lines 432-444 uses only the event-derived pattern with no coordination table query.
smoking_guns.truncation_risk: GetClaimContextTool uses limit: 50 for both claims and releases queries. If a session has more than 50 claim events, older claims are silently invisible, producing incorrect active claims.
smoking_guns.no_conflict_detection: GetClaimContextTool returns activePaths as a flat string list with no conflict metadata. packet.ts assembleL1 at line 208 computes conflictFiles = ownedFiles.filter(f => dirtyFiles.includes(f)) — GetClaimContextTool does not compute this.

changes.description: Remove EventStore import if no longer used by other tools in this file
changes.note: EventStore is also used by QueryEventHistoryTool and GetValidationContextTool, so it must stay

changes.description: Replace GetClaimContextTool execute() body to match the packet.ts pattern: primary path = Coordination.getSessionReservations(), fallback = eventStore.query()
changes.note: The actual code needs an extractFilePaths helper (or inline equivalent). Packet.ts already has extractFilePaths() in lines 119-128 that could be reused if extracted to a shared module.

should_extract_helper.description: packet.ts defines extractFilePaths() locally. To reuse it in tools.ts without duplication, it should be extracted to a shared module (e.g., context/utils.ts or context/claims.ts)
